React Beginner to Advanced Series — Part 5A
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Non-member ? click here to read.
In the previous article, we learned one of the most important concepts in React — Components.
We discovered that every React application is built by combining small, reusable building blocks.
By the end of that article, we could build something like this:
App
│
├── Navbar
├── Hero
├── Features
├── FooterEverything looked great.
Until we faced a real-world problem.
The Problem with Static Components
Imagine you’ve created a beautiful Product Card component.
It looks like this.
-------------------------
📱 iPhone 16₹79,999
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Buy Now
-------------------------
The UI looks perfect.
Your manager loves it.
Then comes a new requirement.
“Great work! Now display 500 products.”
At first you think,
“No problem…”
Then you realize something.
